basic_info.name: Lin Xiaoya
basic_info.occupation: UX Designer at a Major Tech Company
basic_info.education: Bachelor's Degree - Sichuan Fine Arts Institute (Visual Communication Design)

cooking_background.skill_level: Home Cooking Expert (Specializes in fusion cuisine and creative plating)
cooking_background.learning_history: Started self-teaching during university due to nostalgia for hometown flavors. After entering the workforce, influenced by design thinking, she views cooking as 'edible product design' and has systematically studied baking theory and the fundamentals of molecular gastronomy.

family_relationships.name: Zhang Hui
family_relationships.connection_to_cooking: The启蒙 (enlightener) of traditional Sichuan cuisine. She often provides 'cloud guidance' via video call for Xiaoya to make authentic Twice-Cooked Pork. She is the foundation of Xiaoya's taste memory, though she frequently complains that Xiaoya's dishes are 'too exquisite and not hearty enough for rice'.

family_relationships.name: Lin Jianguo
family_relationships.connection_to_cooking: Senior Ingredient Procurement Consultant. Living in suburban Chengdu, he特意 (specifically) visits wholesale markets every week to select the freshest mushrooms and free-range chickens to ship to Xiaoya, serving as her primary source of high-quality ingredients.

social_circle.name: Chen Sisi
social_circle.connection: Product Operations Manager. Xiaoya's 'Number One Taste Tester' and bento buddy. She is responsible for providing the first wave of likes and comments on Xiaoya's dishes on WeChat Moments. The two often explore new restaurants together on weekends to find inspiration.

social_circle.name: Wang Haoran
social_circle.connection: Fitness Coach. They met due to his need for a high-protein, low-fat diet. He often consults Xiaoya on how to make delicious meals with healthy ingredients and occasionally trades imported beef for Xiaoya's homemade whole wheat bread.

daily_habits.activity: 'Clear the Fridge' challenge: improvising a fusion dish using leftover ingredients and recording the recipe in notes.
Visiting the Baijia Wholesale Market in Shuangliu District or the premium supermarket Hema Fresh to purchase core ingredients for the week, strictly screening for produce freshness.
'Healing Moment': Spending two hours slow-cooking a pot of soup or baking desserts. During this time, she listens to no podcasts, focusing solely on the sounds and aromas of the food transforming.

emotional_mapping.emotion: High work stress / Frustrated by client revision requests
emotional_mapping.behavior: Rejects takeout and immediately cooks a bowl of rich, flavorful 'Soul-Stirring Pea and Minced Meat Noodles,' releasing stress through the rhythm of chopping and the spicy taste.

Recreates her mother's signature 'Open-Boiled Cabbage' (simplified version). She feels an emotional connection with her family during the slow-simmering broth process and savors the dish alone upon completion.

Photographs the meticulously plated dishes like a professional shoot, edits the photos, and posts them on Xiaohongshu (Little Red Book). She replies to every comment, gaining a sense of community validation.

life_milestones.event: First year living alone. Unable to adapt to the high oil and salt content of takeout, she decided to renovate her rental kitchen, spending 20,000 RMB on professional equipment, officially embarking on a journey of systematic self-taught cooking.

Hand-made 50 portions of 'Sichuan-Style Foie Gras' as gifts for the company annual meeting. Receiving acclaim from the entire company, she established her persona as the 'God of Cooking among Designers,' boosting her professional confidence.
Successfully tackled the complex 'Beef Wellington.' Although she failed once during the process, the final product was perfect, marking the mature fusion of her baking and Western culinary skills.

preferences.cooking_style: Prioritizes both aesthetics and taste. Prefers a healthy, modified style that is 'low in oil and salt but rich in flavor profiles.' Places extreme importance on the color composition of plating and the pairing of tableware.
preferences.shopping: Visits large agricultural wholesale markets on weekends for fresh produce (pursuing ultimate freshness); buys seasonings and urgent ingredients via Hema or JD Daojia on weekday evenings after work.
preferences.sharing: Heavy social media sharer (Xiaohongshu/WeChat Moments). Enjoys bringing exquisite bentos for close colleagues but refuses to post pictures in large public group chats to avoid causing discomfort.
preferences.dining_out: Prefers 'Chef's Table (Omakase)' or hidden 'Bistros.' Focuses on ingredient origin and culinary creativity. Has little interest in trendy spots with long queues. Habitually writes detailed long-form reviews on Dianping.
